name: United States Antimony Corporation

profile.description: United States Antimony mines and processes antimony, zeolite, and precious metals in Montana and Mexico. Antimony is a critical mineral for defense, batteries, and semiconductor applications. Revenue declined 3.1% YoY with significant losses. D/E of 0.15% reflects minimal leverage. Headquartered in Thompson Falls, Montana.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (0/3): No significant innovation or business model transformation. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI. Market Share (2/2): Only domestic antimony producer in the U.S. — a unique strategic position given Chinese export controls on critical minerals. Product Pipeline (1/2): Government interest in domestic antimony sourcing. R&D Intensity (0/1): Minimal.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Domestic critical mineral supply for national security. Community Impact (1/2): Montana employer. Employee Retention (0/2): Small operation. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Revenue declined 3.1% with profit margin of -41.4% and ROE of -19.7% [1]. D/E of 0.15% is excellent (score 1.0). All other metrics score poorly. $1B market cap on minimal revenue.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Mining and mineral processing are physical operations AI cannot substitute. Antimony is essential for AI-related semiconductor manufacturing. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI semiconductor demand increases antimony requirements.

infinite_game.summary: Small mining operation focused on survival and production expansion rather than transformative purpose.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Domestic critical mineral security is genuine purpose but the company is too small to meaningfully advance it.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): Very small operation (~100 employees), limited data.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): No competitive awareness evident.

investment_thesis: UAMY is the only domestic U.S. antimony producer, positioned to benefit from Chinese export controls on critical minerals and government interest in supply chain reshoring. D/E of 0.15% provides balance sheet safety.
thesis_continued: Revenue declining 3.1% with -41.4% profit margin and -19.7% ROE reflect a company struggling to achieve commercial viability despite favorable policy tailwinds [1]. $1B market cap on minimal revenue is highly speculative.
key_catalysts: Chinese antimony export controls benefiting domestic production
Antimony demand from defense and semiconductor applications
key_risks: Persistent losses and declining revenue despite favorable policy environment
$1B market cap on minimal revenue is highly speculative
Small scale operations (~100 employees) limit ability to scale even with government support
sources: Yahoo Finance — UAMY Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
